app.name: Kyma CLI
app.one_liner: A command-line interface tool designed to simplify the use of Kyma for application developers by managing modules and deploying applications with minimal commands.

screens.name: CLI Command Interface
screens.purpose: Provide a terminal-based interface for users to execute Kyma operations such as app deployment, module management, and cluster authorization.
primary_actions: Manage community modules with 'kyma module pull/add/delete'

rust_commands.name: alpha_authorize
rust_commands.purpose: Grant unified access to Kyma clusters for users, groups, subaccounts, and GitHub repositories (alpha feature).

rust_commands.name: alpha_diagnose_logs
rust_commands.purpose: Retrieve error logs from modules or components for Day 2 diagnostics (alpha feature).

rust_commands.name: app_push
rust_commands.purpose: Deploy and optionally expose a containerized application on a Kyma cluster using a single command.

rust_commands.name: module_pull
rust_commands.purpose: Pull community module templates (e.g., docker-registry) into the default namespace for later installation.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add alpha 'authorize' command with basic help text
week 1: Implement core 'app push' command with exposure support
week 1: Include diagnostic log retrieval for modules
week 1: Integrate module pull/add/delete workflow for community templates
acceptance_tests: User can pull a community module template (e.g., docker-registry), install it with a default CR, and delete it successfully
User can retrieve error logs for a failing module using 'kyma alpha diagnose logs'
User can run 'kyma app push --name test-app --image httpbin --expose --container-port 80' and access the app at https://test-app.{CLUSTER_DOMAIN}/
User receives actionable help when running 'kyma alpha authorize --help', despite it being an alpha command
